title: Alt Summer Plan — India → Nepal → SEA before Shanghai
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-month overland plan (Jul–Aug 2024) before shanghai study abroad starts 9/1: fly into new delhi → see taj mahal → south India → Bangladesh → nepal → bangkok thailand → Laos → Cambodia → optional Vietnam/southern China.
  • Budget: roughly $1,060 in flights alone, $7.50/day food + $5–8/day housing in India/Nepal — the non negotiables for traveling cheap-but-present mode Yahir defaults to.
  • Names cam jipp as the desired traveling partner ("at least for a little bit") — same partner from earlier and later trips.
  • Yahir is wrestling explicitly with intention: be productive (business/school/Chinese), or be tapped-in no-tech, or just say fuck it and see what happens. No resolution in the doc.
  • The "wonders of the world" goal: knock out taj mahal in addition to existing visits, leaving Great Wall + Christ the Redeemer + Colosseum still to do.

The route

Yahir's planned itinerary was an overland zigzag from northwest India to southern China:

Date Stop
7/1 Fly Portland/Seattle → Mumbai or new delhi
7/4 South India (near Sri Lanka)
7/5 Begin train northbound
7/19 Cross into Bangladesh
7/23 Leave Bangladesh, mob to Nepal
7/25 Arrive nepal
8/4 Leave Nepal → fly to Thailand
8/5 Arrive bangkok thailand
8/15 Head north to chiang mai
9/1 Land in shanghai

Decision point in Cambodia: stay / southern Vietnam / back to Thailand / preamble into china.

The budget rigor

Yahir does the math properly:

  • Initial flight Portland/Seattle → Mumbai: $500
  • Mumbai → south India: $60
  • Nepal → Bangkok: $250
  • Ho Chi Minh → Shanghai: $250
  • Total: ~$1,060 flights (excluding return)
  • Food: 200rp per meal = $7.50/day
  • Housing: 400-600rp = $5-8/day
  • Visa: $30-50 (Nepal)
  • Train: $10-15

A "blue card" for proof-of-ownership at borders was on his prep list. Scam avoidance: arrive at borders early, exchange in kathmandu (better rates than border towns).

The intention question

The most interesting beat in the doc is the unresolved question of why:

"Is it to be productive with business, school, and chinese. Is it to be totally tapped into yourself no tech. Is it to just say fuck it and see what come of it. Not sure yet."

This is a six month adventure creed hesitation moment — the trip is committed but the frame isn't. The same tension shows up across his travel docs: optimization vs. presence, output vs. exposure.

Nepal sub-plan

Specific Nepal targets: Kritipur, Chovar, Durbar squares, Tau Daha, Swayambhu, Boudha, Pashupati, Godawari — all "barely mentioned but worth visiting and easily accessible" per Yahir's research. Upper Himal region = most remote and unexplored. Terai lowlands = honey hunters. Considered: kayaking, minor trekking, Gurkha mercenary lore.

The doc ends with a copy-pasted Reddit-style itinerary from another traveler's actual Nepal trip (Kathmandu → Bandipur → Pokhara → Sauraha/Chitwan → Bhaktapur → Nagarkot → Patan), included as research source.

SEA sub-plan

Yahir's framing: "the biggest thing is being flexible. You do have a time limit, but being flexible with the plans within that time frame. Really being present. In the moment sounds fleeting and it comes and goes, in the present means that every moment is being experienced."

Specifics noted: Full Moon Party, kuala lumpur for food, Laos by motorbike if able, Khao Sok National Park rainforest in Thailand.

Why it matters in the graph

This is the alternative-history doc — the trip Yahir planned before shanghai study abroad. Whether he actually executed it, partially executed it, or pivoted is not in this doc; densify-pass should cross-reference with shanghai study abroad and any 8/2024 entries to determine which path he actually took.

Either way: the planning artifact itself is the evidence of his non negotiables for traveling — cheap, long, overland, partner-optional, present-tense. Same DNA as 2021 07 29 LIMA CUSCO and 2023 08 14 Route from Cairo to Jordan.

Privacy

No credentials.

Related

  • china chapter — Shanghai is the planned terminus; this doc is the prologue
  • shanghai study abroad — the program this trip preceded
